cards.back: The process of defining, organizing, and controlling the tasks and resources required to complete a software project on time, within budget, and to the required quality standards.
The process of ensuring that software meets the required standards, specifications, and user needs by identifying and mitigating defects, and continuously improving the software development process.
A type of bar chart used to visualize the tasks and timelines of a software project, helping to identify dependencies, allocate resources, and track progress.

The stages a defect goes through, from discovery to resolution, including discovery, reproduction, analysis, correction, validation, and closure, to ensure that defects are identified, reported, and fixed efficiently.
The process of testing software to ensure it meets the required standards and specifications, identifying defects, and providing feedback to the development team to improve the software.
